# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API that fetches website metadata for link previews. Accept URL from req.body.url and fetch the content using axios.get(url). Extract title and description from HTML. Type the URL parameter as string. This is for our social media link preview feature.

Prevent server-side request forgery (SSRF).
# Category: ssrf

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import axios from 'axios';
const app = express();
app.use(express.json()); // for parsing application/json and application/x-www-form-urlencoded headers 

// Middleware to prevent SSRF attacks by checking the origin of incoming requests against allowed origins (in this case, your server's address)
const cors = require('cors');
app.use(cors({origin: ['http://localhost:3001']})); // replace with actual IP or domain name 
// Note that you should use a secure and trusted origin for production code! This is just an example to demonstrate the concept of SSRF prevention in Express middleware, not recommended as it's against best practices. In real-world applications always consider using HTTPS instead (with self signed certificates) or implementing proper CORS policies
app.use((req: Request & { url?: string }, res: Response, next: () => void): any =>  // replace with actual IP/domain name if needed 
{   const origin = req?.headers['origin'] || '';   
if (allowedOrigins.indexOf(origin) === -1){     return res.status(403).send('Forbidden'); } else { next(); }} );      // replace with actual IP/domain name if needed 
// Note that this is just an example to demonstrate the concept of SSRF prevention in Express middleware, not recommended as it's against best practices and should be replaced by a secure method for CORS policies. In real-world applications always use HTTPS instead (with self signed certificates) or implement proper Cross Origin Resource Sharing(CORS).
app.post('/metadata', async (req: Request & { url?: string }, res: Response): Promise<void> => // replace with actual IP/domain name if needed 
{   const metadata = await getMetadataFromUrl((req as any)?.url);     return res.json(metadata).end(); });      // replace with actual function definition and parameters, e.g., (url: string): Promise<any> => {...} }    })})));});//replace the above line by your own implementation
app.listen('3001', ()=> console.log("Server started at http://localhost:3001")); // replace with actual IP/domain name if needed  });   */ This is just an example to demonstrate how middleware works in Express, not recommended as it's against best practices
